title: Tanacetum hissaricum
intro: Tanacetum hissaricum, also known as Hissar tansy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum hissaricum include flavonoids, coumarins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Tanacetum hissaricum is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments, to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders and menstrual issues.
taxonomy: Tanacetum hissaricum belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a genus of flowering plants within the daisy family. The classification of Tanacetum hissaricum is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Asterales. Its classification is a reflection of its botanical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships to other plants. Tanacetum hissaricum is a member of the Compositae clade, a subgroup within the Asteraceae family.
